property.name: java.library.path
property.value: D:\Development\Tools\Java\jdk-17\bin;C:\Windows\Sun\Java\bin;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;C:\Python314\Scripts\;C:\Python314\;C:\Program Files\Common Files\Oracle\Java\javapath;C:\Program Files (x86)\NVIDIA Corporation\PhysX\Common;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;C:\Windows\System32\Wbem;C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\dotnet\;D:\Development\Tools\Java\jdk-17\bin;D:\Development\Tools\Maven\apache-maven-3.9.14\bin;D:\Development\Tools\Android\adk-3;D:\Development\Tools\nodejs\;C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\bin;C:\Users\Gregory Zwane\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\Gregory Zwane\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Users\Gregory Zwane\AppData\Local\Programs\Antigravity\bin;C:\Users\Gregory Zwane\AppData\Roaming\npm;C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition 2025.1.7\bin;;.

testcase.name: runScenario["Register, Login and Place Order for Computer", "Demo Web Shop E-commerce Testing"](1)

failure.message: no such element: Unable to locate element: {"method":"css selector","selector":"#add\-to\-cart\-button\-1"} (Session info: chrome=147.0.7727.117) For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#no-such-element-exception Build info: version: '4.15.0', revision: '1d14b5521b' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '17.0.17' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.chrome.ChromeDriver Command: [9539cda2c7f067e4af89de576c59a2fe, findElement {using=id, value=add-to-cart-button-1}] Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: false, browserName: chrome, browserVersion: 147.0.7727.117, chrome: {chromedriverVersion: 147.0.7727.117 (474cc805edd..., userDataDir: C:\Users\GREGOR~1\AppData\L...}, fedcm:accounts: true, goog:chromeOptions: {debuggerAddress: localhost:50099}, goog:processID: 19504, networkConnectionEnabled: false, pageLoadStrategy: normal, platformName: windows, proxy: Proxy(), se:cdp: ws://localhost:50099/devtoo..., se:cdpVersion: 147.0.7727.117, setWindowRect: true, strictFileInteractability: false, timeouts: {implicit: 0, pageLoad: 300000, script: 30000}, unhandledPromptBehavior: dismiss and notify, webauthn:extension:credBlob: true, webauthn:extension:largeBlob: true, webauthn:extension:minPinLength: true, webauthn:extension:prf: true, webauthn:virtualAuthenticators: true} Session ID: 9539cda2c7f067e4af89de576c59a2fe

testcase.system-out:
@Regression Scenario: Register, Login and Place Order for Computer # src/test/resources/features/demowebshop.feature:7 Given User is on the Demo Web Shop home page # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.HomeSteps.user_is_on_the_demo_web_shop_home_page() When User navigates to registration page # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.RegistrationSteps.userNavigatesToRegistrationPage() And User registers with new credentials # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.RegistrationSteps.userRegistersWithNewCredentials() Then User should see registration success message # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.RegistrationSteps.userShouldSeeRegistrationSuccessMessage() When User logs out # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.userLogsOut() And User logs in with registered credentials # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.userLogsInWithRegisteredCredentials() Then User should be logged in successfully # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.userShouldBeLoggedInSuccessfully() When User navigates to Computers category # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userNavigatesToComputersCategory() And User selects Building Computers # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userSelectsBuildingComputers() And User adds a computer to cart # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userAddsAComputerToCart() org.openqa.selenium.NoSuchElementException: no such element: Unable to locate element: {"method":"css selector","selector":"#add\-to\-cart\-button\-1"} (Session info: chrome=147.0.7727.117) For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#no-such-element-exception Build info: version: '4.15.0', revision: '1d14b5521b' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: Then User should see item added to cart # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userShouldSeeItemAddedToCart() When User proceeds to checkout # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userProceedsToCheckout() And User fills billing and shipping information # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userFillsBillingAndShippingInformation() And User places the order # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userPlacesTheOrder() Then User should see order confirmation # com.demowebshop.stepDefinitions.OrderSteps.userShouldSeeOrderConfirmation()
testcase.system-err: [main] INFO io.github.bonigarcia.wdm.WebDriverManager - Using chromedriver 147.0.7727.117 (resolved driver for Chrome 147) [main] INFO io.github.bonigarcia.wdm.WebDriverManager - Exporting webdriver.chrome.driver as C:\Users\Gregory Zwane\.cache\selenium\chromedriver\win64\147.0.7727.117\chromedriver.exe Apr 26, 2026 6:31:33 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find CDP implementation matching 147 Apr 26, 2026 6:31:33 PM org.openqa.selenium.chromium.ChromiumDriver lambda$new$5 WARNING: Unable to find version of CDP to use for 147.0.7727.117. You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.15.0` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's.
